_comment: Claude Video Editing Flow explainer — SSL 2026 conference handover. Per-LINE generation: each sentence is its own TTS call, chained via request-id. Files: {scene}__{line}.mp3 → stitched per-scene → final listen-through. Finder colour-tag review (blue=keep, red=regen, green=mid) drives selective regen. Em-dashes used as TTS breath markers ONLY (never read by humans, never copied to mailers/READMEs/UI).

lines.text: Editing long-form video by hand — used to mean scrubbing a four-minute take to find the sixty seconds worth keeping.
What if you could read the cuts as a list — and just tick the ones that work.
To get started — drop in any video file.
Claude transcribes it word-by-word — ranks every quotable segment — and prints a coloured terminal table.
Next stage — Claude scores every segment against your selection rules.
Five pipeline stages — two pre-render gates — every cut audited before render.
Then you reply with a combo letter — or a number list.
Claude renders. Single grade — thirty-millisecond fades — clean concat.
Tested on podcast cuts — Loom walkthroughs — vertical reels — and keynote footage.
End-to-end — outside revisions — takes around five minutes.
Two minutes reading. Three minutes Claude renders.
Pull the repo — and put Claude to work.
